old_claim.note: Joint-Stock Insurance Pools provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Barbon's 1680 Fire Office organized fire insurance as a joint-stock office with policy terms and payouts, providing the institutional pool that made repeat claims assessment operational.

source_shape.source_locator: The Insurance Museum describes Barbon's Fire Office as the first joint-stock insurance company in 1680 and quotes early policy terms promising payment if an insured house burned down.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source ties the 1680 joint-stock Fire Office to fire policies and payout terms for burned insured houses.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: That institutional risk pool makes repeat fire-insurance claims assessment operational, while avoiding a claim that every later claims office required this exact London company form.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: joint_stock_insurance_pools remains an enabling edge, now with source-locatable rationale.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened back to all maritime and property claims adjustment rather than early London fire-insurance assessment.
A stronger source showed the scoped claims-assessment practice emerged outside institutional fire-insurance pools.
The joint_stock_insurance_pools node were narrowed to exclude Barbon's 1680 Fire Office.
short_reason: The source-backed 1680 Fire Office gives the claims-assessment node an institutional insurance-pool anchor.
